entries.description: TIER: 1, TRAIT: Agility, RANGE: Melee, DAMAGE: d8 phy, BURDEN: One-Handed, FEATURE: Quick: When you make an attack, you can mark a Stress to target another creature within range.

entries.name: Improved Light-Frame Wheelchair
entries.description: TIER: 2, TRAIT: Agility, RANGE: Melee, DAMAGE: d8+3 phy, BURDEN: One-Handed, FEATURE: Quick: When you make an attack, you can mark a Stress to target another creature within range.

entries.name: Advanced Light-Frame Wheelchair
entries.description: TIER: 3, TRAIT: Agility, RANGE: Melee, DAMAGE: d8+6 phy, BURDEN: One-Handed, FEATURE: Quick: When you make an attack, you can mark a Stress to target another creature within range.

entries.name: Legendary Light-Frame Wheelchair
entries.description: TIER: 4, TRAIT: Agility, RANGE: Melee, DAMAGE: d8+9 phy, BURDEN: One-Handed, FEATURE: Quick: When you make an attack, you can mark a Stress to target another creature within range.

entries.name: Heavy-Frame Wheelchair
entries.description: TIER: 1, TRAIT: Strength, RANGE: Melee, DAMAGE: d12+3 phy, BURDEN: Two-Handed, FEATURE: Heavy: -1 to Evasion

entries.name: Improved Heavy-Frame Wheelchair
entries.description: TIER: 2, TRAIT: Strength, RANGE: Melee, DAMAGE: d12+6 phy, BURDEN: Two-Handed, FEATURE: Heavy: -1 to Evasion

entries.name: Advanced Heavy-Frame Wheelchair
entries.description: TIER: 3, TRAIT: Strength, RANGE: Melee, DAMAGE: d12+9 phy, BURDEN: Two-Handed, FEATURE: Heavy: -1 to Evasion

entries.name: Legendary Heavy-Frame Wheelchair
entries.description: TIER: 4, TRAIT: Strength, RANGE: Melee, DAMAGE: d12+12 phy, BURDEN: Two-Handed, FEATURE: Heavy: -1 to Evasion

entries.name: Arcane-Frame Wheelchair
entries.description: TIER: 1, TRAIT: Spellcast, RANGE: Far, DAMAGE: d6 mag, BURDEN: One-Handed, FEATURE: Reliable: +1 to attack rolls

entries.name: Improved Arcane-Frame Wheelchair
entries.description: TIER: 2, TRAIT: Spellcast, RANGE: Far, DAMAGE: d6+3 mag, BURDEN: One-Handed, FEATURE: Reliable: +1 to attack rolls

entries.name: Advanced Arcane-Frame Wheelchair
entries.description: TIER: 3, TRAIT: Spellcast, RANGE: Far, DAMAGE: d6+6 mag, BURDEN: One-Handed, FEATURE: Reliable: +1 to attack rolls

entries.name: Legendary Arcane-Frame Wheelchair
entries.description: TIER: 4, TRAIT: Spellcast, RANGE: Far, DAMAGE: d6+9 mag, BURDEN: One-Handed, FEATURE: Reliable: +1 to attack rolls

entries.name: Gambeson Armor
entries.description: BASE THRESHOLDS: 5 / 11, BASE SCORE: 3, FEATURE: Flexible: +1 to Evasion

entries.name: Leather Armor
entries.description: BASE THRESHOLDS: 6 / 13, BASE SCORE: 3

entries.name: Chainmail Armor
entries.description: BASE THRESHOLDS: 7 / 15, BASE SCORE: 4, FEATURE: Heavy: -1 to Evasion

entries.name: Full Plate Armor
entries.description: BASE THRESHOLDS: 8 / 17, BASE SCORE: 4, FEATURE: Very Heavy: -2 to Evasion; -1 to Agility

entries.name: Improved Gambeson Armor
entries.description: BASE THRESHOLDS: 7 / 16, BASE SCORE: 4, FEATURE: Flexible: +1 to Evasion

entries.name: Improved Leather Armor
entries.description: BASE THRESHOLDS: 9 / 20, BASE SCORE: 4

entries.name: Improved Chainmail Armor
entries.description: BASE THRESHOLDS: 11 / 24, BASE SCORE: 5, FEATURE: Heavy: -1 to Evasion

entries.name: Improved Full Plate Armor
entries.description: BASE THRESHOLDS: 13 / 28, BASE SCORE: 5, FEATURE: Very Heavy: -2 to Evasion; -1 to Agility

entries.name: Eldritch Chain Armor
entries.description: BASE THRESHOLDS: 9 / 21, BASE SCORE: 4, FEATURE: Warded: You reduce incoming magic damage by your Armor Score before applying it to your damage thresholds.

entries.name: Harrowbone Armor
entries.description: BASE THRESHOLDS: 9 / 21, BASE SCORE: 4, FEATURE: Resilient: Before you mark your last Armor Slot, roll a d6. On a result of 6, you can clear at least 1 Armor Slot without marking an Armor Slot.

entries.name: Irontree Breastplate Armor
entries.description: BASE THRESHOLDS: 9 / 20, BASE SCORE: 4, FEATURE: Reinforced: When you mark your last Armor Slot, increase your damage thresholds by +2 until you clear at least 1 Armor Slot.

entries.name: Runetan Floating Armor
entries.description: BASE THRESHOLDS: 9 / 20, BASE SCORE: 4, FEATURE: Shifting: When you are targeted for an attack, you can mark an Armor Slot to give the attack roll against you disadvantage.

entries.name: Tyra Soft Armor
entries.description: BASE THRESHOLDS: 8 / 18, BASE SCORE: 5, FEATURE: Quiet: You gain a +2 bonus to rolls you make to move silently.

entries.name: Rosewild Armor
entries.description: BASE THRESHOLDS: 11 / 23, BASE SCORE: 5, FEATURE: Hopeful: When you would spend a Hope, you can mark an Armor Slot instead.

entries.name: Advanced Gambeson Armor
entries.description: BASE THRESHOLDS: 9 / 23, BASE SCORE: 5, FEATURE: Flexible: +1 to Evasion

entries.name: Advanced Leather Armor
entries.description: BASE THRESHOLDS: 13 / 27, BASE SCORE: 5

entries.name: Advanced Chainmail Armor
entries.description: BASE THRESHOLDS: 13 / 31, BASE SCORE: 6, FEATURE: Heavy: -1 to Evasion

entries.name: Advanced Full Plate Armor
entries.description: BASE THRESHOLDS: 15 / 35, BASE SCORE: 6, FEATURE: Very Heavy: -2 to Evasion; -1 to Agility

entries.name: Bahamia Free Armor
entries.description: BASE THRESHOLDS: 11 / 27, BASE SCORE: 5, FEATURE: Gilded: +1 to Presence

entries.name: Dragonscale Armor
entries.description: BASE THRESHOLDS: 11 / 27, BASE SCORE: 5, FEATURE: Impenetrable: Once per short rest, when you would mark your last Hit Point, you can instead mark a Stress.

entries.name: Spiked Plate Armor
entries.description: BASE THRESHOLDS: 10 / 25, BASE SCORE: 5, FEATURE: Sharp: On a successful attack against a target within Melee range, add a d4 to the damage roll.

entries.name: Bladefare Armor
entries.description: BASE THRESHOLDS: 16 / 39, BASE SCORE: 6, FEATURE: Physical: You can't mark an Armor Slot to reduce magic damage.

entries.name: Monast's Cloak
entries.description: BASE THRESHOLDS: 16 / 39, BASE SCORE: 6, FEATURE: Magic: You can't mark an Armor Slot to reduce physical damage.

entries.name: Runes of Fortification
entries.description: BASE THRESHOLDS: 17 / 43, BASE SCORE: 6, FEATURE: Painful: Each time you mark an Armor Slot, you must mark a Stress.

entries.name: Legendary Gambeson Armor
entries.description: BASE THRESHOLDS: 11 / 32, BASE SCORE: 6, FEATURE: Flexible: +1 to Evasion

entries.name: Legendary Leather Armor
entries.description: BASE THRESHOLDS: 13 / 36, BASE SCORE: 6

entries.name: Legendary Chainmail Armor
entries.description: BASE THRESHOLDS: 15 / 40, BASE SCORE: 7, FEATURE: Heavy: -1 to Evasion

entries.name: Legendary Full Plate Armor
entries.description: BASE THRESHOLDS: 17 / 44, BASE SCORE: 7, FEATURE: Very Heavy: -2 to Evasion; -1 to Agility

entries.name: Dynama Silkchain
entries.description: BASE THRESHOLDS: 13 / 36, BASE SCORE: 7, FEATURE: Timeslowing: Mark an Armor Slot to roll a d8 and add its result as a bonus to your Evasion against an incoming attack.

entries.name: Channeling Armor
entries.description: BASE THRESHOLDS: 13 / 36, BASE SCORE: 5, FEATURE: Channeling: -1 to Spellcast Rolls

entries.name: Emberwoven Armor
entries.description: BASE THRESHOLDS: 13 / 36, BASE SCORE: 6, FEATURE: Burning: When an adversary attacks you within Melee range, they mark a Stress.

entries.name: Full Fortified Armor
entries.description: BASE THRESHOLDS: 15 / 40, BASE SCORE: 4, FEATURE: Fortified: When you mark an Armor Slot, you reduce the severity of an attack by two thresholds instead of one.

entries.name: Veritas Opal Armor
entries.description: BASE THRESHOLDS: 13 / 36, BASE SCORE: 6, FEATURE: Truthseeking: This armor glows when another creature within Close range tells a lie.

entries.name: Savior Chainmail
entries.description: BASE THRESHOLDS: 18 / 48, BASE SCORE: 8, FEATURE: Difficult: -1 to all character traits and Evasion

entries.name: Premium Bedroll
entries.description: During downtime, you automatically clear a Stress.

entries.name: Piper Whistle
entries.description: This instrument makes a distinctive sound. When you blow this whistle, its piercing tone can be heard within a 1-mile radius.

entries.name: Charging Quiver
entries.description: When you succeed on an attack with an arrow stored in this quiver, gain a bonus to the damage roll equal to your current tier.

entries.name: Alasair's Torch
entries.description: You can light this magic torch at will. The flame's light fills a much larger space than it should, enough to illuminate a cave bright as day.

entries.name: Speaking Orbs
entries.description: This pair of orbs allows any creatures holding them to communicate with each other across any distance.

entries.name: Manacles
entries.description: This pair of locking cuffs comes with a key.

entries.name: Arcane Cloak
entries.description: A creature with a Spellcast trait wearing this cloak can adjust its color, texture, and size at will.

entries.name: Woven Net
entries.description: You can make a Finesse Roll using this net to trap a small creature. A trapped target can break free with a successful Attack Roll (16).

entries.name: Fire Jar
entries.description: When opened, the magical liquid contents of this jar to instantly produce fire. The contents regenerate when you take a long rest.

entries.name: Suspended Rod
entries.description: This rod is embedded with runes. When you activate the rod, it is immediately suspended in place. Until the rod is deactivated, it can't move, doesn't abide by the rules of gravity, and remains in place.

entries.name: Glamour Stone
entries.description: Activate this goblin-sized stone to memorize the appearance of someone you can see. Spend a Hope to magically recreate this guise on yourself as an illusion.

entries.name: Empty Chest
entries.description: This magical chest appears empty. When you speak a specific trigger word or action and open the chest, you can see the items stored within it.

entries.name: Companion Case
entries.description: This case can fit a small animal companion. While the companion is inside, the animal can see any immune to all damage and harmful effects.

entries.name: Piercing Arrows
entries.description: Three times per rest when you succeed on an attack with one of these arrows, you can add your Proficiency to the damage roll.

entries.name: Valorstone
entries.description: You can attach this stone to armor that doesn't already have a feature. The armor gains the following feature. Resilient: Before you mark your last Armor Slot, roll a d6. On a result of 6, reduce the severity by one threshold without marking an Armor Slot.

entries.name: Skeleton Key
entries.description: When you use this key to open a locked door, you gain advantage on the Finesse Roll.

entries.name: Arcane Prism
entries.description: Position this prism in a location of your choosing and activate it. All allies within Close range of it gain a +1 bonus to their Spellcast Rolls. While activated, the prism can't be moved. Once the prism is deactivated, it can't be activated again until your next long rest.

entries.name: Minor Stamina Potion Recipe
entries.description: As a downtime move, you can use the bone of a creature to craft a Minor Stamina Potion.

entries.name: Minor Health Potion Recipe
entries.description: As a downtime move, you can use a vial of blood to craft a Minor Health Potion.

entries.name: Hunting Compasses
entries.description: These two compasses point toward each other no matter how far apart they are.

entries.name: Corrective Sprite
entries.description: This tiny sprite sits in the curve of your ear canal and whispers helpful advice during combat. Once per short rest, you can gain advantage on an attack roll.

entries.name: Gecko Gloves
entries.description: You can climb up vertical surfaces and across ceilings.

entries.name: Lorekeeper
entries.description: You can store the name and details of up to three hostile creatures inside this book. You gain a +1 bonus to action rolls against those creatures.

entries.name: Vial of Darksmoke Recipe
entries.description: As a downtime move, you can mark a Stress to craft a Vial of Darksmoke.

entries.name: Bloodstone
entries.description: You can attach this stone to a weapon that doesn't already have a feature. The weapon gains the following feature. Brutal: When you roll the maximum value on a damage die, roll an additional damage die.

entries.name: Greatstone
entries.description: You can attach this stone to a weapon that doesn't already have a feature. The weapon gains the following feature. Cleaving: On a successful attack, roll an additional damage die and discard the lowest result.

entries.name: Glider
entries.description: While falling, you can mark a Stress to deploy this silk parachute and glide safely to the ground.

entries.name: Ring of Silence
entries.description: Spend a Hope to activate this ring. Your footsteps are silent until your next rest.

entries.name: Calming Charm
entries.description: When you would mark your very last Stress, roll a d6. On a result of 5 or higher, don't mark it.

entries.name: Dual Flask
entries.description: This flask can hold two different liquids. You can swap between them by flipping a small switch on the flask's side.

entries.name: Bag of Ficklsand
entries.description: You can convince this small bag of sand to be much heavier or lighter with a successful Presence Roll (10). Additionally, on a successful Finesse Roll (10), you can blow a bit of sand into a target's face to make them temporarily Vulnerable.

entries.name: Ring of Resistance
entries.description: Once per long rest, you can activate this ring after a successful attack against you to halve the damage.

entries.name: Phoenix Feather
entries.description: If you have at least one Phoenix Feather on you when you fall unconscious, you gain a +1 bonus to the roll you make to determine whether you gain a scar.

entries.name: Box of Many Goods
entries.description: Once per long rest, you can open this small box and roll a d12. On a result of 1–6, it's empty. On a result of 7–10, it contains one random common consumable. On a result of 11–12, it contains two random common consumables.

entries.name: Archide Charm
entries.description: You can attach this charm to a weapon with a Melee range. Three times per rest, you can activate the charm and attack a target within Close range.

entries.name: Portal Seed
entries.description: Spend a Hope to activate this seed and grow a portal in that spot. The portal is ready to use in 24 hours. You can use this portal to travel to any other location where you planted a portal seed. A portal can be destroyed by dealing any amount of magic damage.

entries.name: Paragon's Chain
entries.description: As a downtime move, you can meditate on an ideal or principle you hold dear and focus your will into this chain. Once per long rest, you can spend a Hope to roll a d20 as your Hope Die (or rolls that directly align with that principle.

entries.name: Elusive Amulet
entries.description: Once per long rest, you can activate this amulet to become Hidden until you move. While Hidden in this way, you remain unseen even if an adversary moves to where they would normally see you.

entries.name: Hopekeeper Locket
entries.description: During a long rest, if you have 0 Hope, you can spend a Hope to imbue this locket with your bountiful resolve. When you have 0 Hope, you can use the locket to immediately gain a Hope. The locket must be re-imbued before it can be activated again.

entries.name: Infinite Bag
entries.description: When you store items in this bag, they are kept in a pocket dimension that never runs out of space. You can retrieve an item as any time.

entries.name: Stride Relic
entries.description: You gain a +1 bonus to your Agility. You can only carry one relic.

entries.name: Bolder Relic
entries.description: You gain a +1 bonus to your Strength. You can only carry one relic.

entries.name: Control Relic
entries.description: You gain a +1 bonus to your Finesse. You can only carry one relic.

entries.name: Attune Relic
entries.description: You gain a +1 bonus to your Instinct. You can only carry one relic.

entries.name: Charm Relic
entries.description: You gain a +1 bonus to your Presence. You can only carry one relic.

entries.name: Enlighten Relic
entries.description: You gain a +1 bonus to your Knowledge. You can only carry one relic.

entries.name: Honing Relic
entries.description: You gain a +1 bonus to an Experience of your choice. You can only carry one relic.

entries.name: Flickerfly Pendant
entries.description: While you carry this pendant, your weapons with a Melee range that deal physical damage have a gossamer sheen and can attack targets within Very Close range.

entries.name: Lakestrider Boots
entries.description: You can walk on the surface of water as if it were soft ground.

entries.name: Clay Companion
entries.description: When you sculpt this ball of clay into a clay animal companion, it behaves as that animal. For example, a clay spider can spin webs, while a clay bird can fly. The clay companion retains memory and identity across different shapes, but they can adopt new mannerisms with each form.

entries.name: Mythic Dust Recipe
entries.description: As a downtime move, you can use a handful of fine gold dust to craft Mythic Dust.

entries.name: Shard of Memory
entries.description: Once per long rest, you can spend 2 Hope to recall a domain card from your vault instead of paying its Recall Cost.

entries.name: Gem of Alacrity
entries.description: You can attach this gem to a weapon, allowing you to use your Agility when making an attack with that weapon.

entries.name: Gem of Might
entries.description: You can attach this gem to a weapon, allowing you to use your Strength when making an attack with that weapon.

entries.name: Gem of Precision
entries.description: You can attach this gem to a weapon, allowing you to use your Finesse when making an attack with that weapon.

entries.name: Gem of Insight
entries.description: You can attach this gem to a weapon, allowing you to use your Instinct when making an attack with that weapon.

entries.name: Gem of Audacity
entries.description: You can attach this gem to a weapon, allowing you to use your Presence when making an attack with that weapon.

entries.name: Gem of Sagacity
entries.description: You can attach this gem to a weapon, allowing you to use your Knowledge when making an attack with that weapon.

entries.name: Ring of Unbreakable Memory
entries.description: Once per session, when the GM spends a Fear, you can spend 4 Hope to cancel the effects of that spent Fear.

entries.name: Belt of Unity
entries.description: Once per session, you can spend 5 Hope to lead a Tag Team Roll with three PCs instead of two.

entries.name: Stride Potion
entries.description: You gain a +1 bonus to your next Agility Roll.

entries.name: Bolster Potion
entries.description: You gain a +1 bonus to your next Strength Roll.

entries.name: Control Potion
entries.description: You gain a +1 bonus to your next Finesse Roll.

entries.name: Attune Potion
entries.description: You gain a +1 bonus to your next Instinct Roll.

entries.name: Charm Potion
entries.description: You gain a +1 bonus to your next Presence Roll.

entries.name: Enlighten Potion
entries.description: You gain a +1 bonus to your next Knowledge Roll.

entries.name: Minor Health
entries.description: Clear 1d4 HP.

entries.name: Minor Stamina Potion
entries.description: Clear 1d4 Stress.

entries.name: Grindletooth Venom
entries.description: You can apply this venom to a weapon that deals physical damage to add a d6 to your next damage roll with that weapon.

entries.name: Vark Leaves
entries.description: You can eat these pared leaves to immediately regain 2 Hope.

entries.name: Vial of Moondrip
entries.description: When you drink the contents of this vial, you can see in total darkness until your next rest.

entries.name: Unstable Arcane Shard
entries.description: You can make a Finesse Roll to throw this shard at a group of adversaries within Far range. Targets you succeed against take 1d10 magic damage.

entries.name: Potion of Stability
entries.description: You can drink this potion to choose one additional downtime move.

entries.name: Improved Grindletooth Venom
entries.description: You can apply this venom to a weapon that deals physical damage to add a d10 to your next damage roll with that weapon.

entries.name: Morphing Clay
entries.description: You can spend a Hope to use this clay, altering your face enough to make you unrecognizable until your next rest.

entries.name: Vial of Darksmoke
entries.description: When an adversary attacks you, use this vial and roll a number of d6s equal to your Agility. Add the highest result. If you roll all 1s, mark a Stress.

entries.name: Jumping Root
entries.description: Eat this root to leap up to Far range once without needing to roll.

entries.name: Snap Powder
entries.description: Mark a Stress and clear a HP.

entries.name: Health Potion
entries.description: Clear 1d8+1 HP.

entries.name: Stamina Potion
entries.description: Clear 1d8+1 Stress.

entries.name: Armor Stitcher
entries.description: You can use this stitcher to spend any number of Hope to clear that many Armor Slots.

entries.name: Gill Salve
entries.description: You can apply this salve to your neck to breathe underwater for a number of minutes equal to your level.

entries.name: Replication Parchment
entries.description: By touching this piece of parchment to another, you can perfectly copy that document's contents. Once used, this parchment becomes mundane paper.

entries.name: Improved Arcane Shard
entries.description: You can make a Finesse Roll to throw this shard at a group of adversaries within Far range. Targets you succeed against take 2d6 magic damage.

entries.name: Major Stride Potion
entries.description: You gain a +1 bonus to your Agility until your next rest.

entries.name: Major Bolster Potion
entries.description: You gain a +1 bonus to your Strength until your next rest.

entries.name: Major Control Potion
entries.description: You gain a +1 bonus to your Finesse until your next rest.

entries.name: Major Attune Potion
entries.description: You gain a +1 bonus to your Instinct until your next rest.

entries.name: Major Charm Potion
entries.description: You gain a +1 bonus to your Presence until your next rest.

entries.name: Major Enlighten Potion
entries.description: You gain a +1 bonus to your Knowledge until your next rest.

entries.name: Blood Leaves of Yorgi
entries.description: You can eat these leaves to teleport away from where you are and immediately teleport at a point you can see within Very Far range.

entries.name: Hornest's Secret Potion
entries.description: After drinking this potion, the next successful attack you make critically succeeds.

entries.name: Redhorm Saliva
entries.description: You can apply this saliva to a weapon that deals physical damage to add a d12 to your next damage roll with that weapon.

entries.name: Chamelstone
entries.description: You can use this stone to take a spell or grimoire from your vault, use it once, and return it to your vault.

entries.name: Mythic Dust
entries.description: You can apply this dust to a weapon that doesn't already have a feature and add a +1 to your next damage roll with that weapon.

entries.name: Acidpaste
entries.description: This paste eats away walls and other surfaces in bright flashes.

entries.name: Hopehold Flare
entries.description: When you use this flare, allies within Close range roll a d6 when they spend Hope. On a result of 4–6, they gain the effect of that Hope without spending it. The flare lasts until the end of the scene.

entries.name: Major Arcane Shard
entries.description: You can make a Finesse Roll to throw this shard at a group of adversaries within Far range. Targets you succeed against take 4d20 magic damage.

entries.name: Featherbone
entries.description: You can use this bone to control your falling speed for a number of minutes equal to your level.

entries.name: Circle of the Void
entries.description: Mark a Stress to create a void that extends up to Far range. No magic can be cast inside the void, and creatures inside the void are immune to magic damage.

entries.name: Sun Tree Sap
entries.description: Consume this sap to roll a d6. On a result of 5–6, clear 2 HP. On a result of 2–4, clear 1 Stress. On a result of 1, see through the veil of death and return a spirit gaining one scar.

entries.name: DripFang Poison
entries.description: A creature who consumes this poison takes 2d6 over time magic damage.

entries.name: Major Health Potion
entries.description: Clear 1d4+2 HP.

entries.name: Major Stamina Potion
entries.description: Clear 1d4+2 Stress.

entries.name: Ogre Musk
entries.description: You can spread this musk to prevent anyone from tracking you by mundane or magical means until your next rest.

entries.name: Wingsprout
entries.description: You gain magic wings that allow you to fly for a number of minutes equal to your level.

entries.name: Jar of Lost Voices
entries.description: You can open this jar to release a deafening echo of voices for a number of minutes equal to your level. All creatures within Close range are unprepared for the sound take 6d8 magic damage.

entries.name: Dragonbloom Tea
entries.description: You can drink this tea to unleash a fiery breath attack. Make an Instinct Roll against all adversaries in front of you within Close range. Targets you succeed against take 3d8 fire magic damage using your Proficiency.

entries.name: Bridge Seed
entries.description: Thick vines grow from your location to a point of your choice within Far range, allowing you to climb up or across them. The vines dissipate on your next short rest.

entries.name: Sleeping Sap
entries.description: You can drink this potion to fall asleep for a full night's rest. You clear all Stress upon waking.

entries.name: Feast of Xuria
entries.description: You can eat this potion to clear all HP and Stress and gain 1d4 Hope.

entries.name: Bonding Honey
entries.description: This honey can be used to glue two objects together permanently.

entries.name: Shrinking Potion
entries.description: You can drink this potion to halve your size until you take damage or choose to grow or your next rest. While in this form, you have a +2 bonus to Agility and a -1 penalty to your Proficiency.

entries.name: Growing Potion
entries.description: You can drink this potion to double your size until you choose to drop this form on your next rest. While in this form, you have a +2 bonus to Strength and a -1 penalty to Agility.

entries.name: Knowledge Stone
entries.description: If you die while holding this stone, an ally can take a card from your loadout to place in their loadout or vault. After they take this knowledge, the stone crumbles.

entries.name: Sweet Moss
entries.description: You can consume this moss during a rest to clear 1d10 HP or 1d10 Stress.

entries.name: Blinding Orb
entries.description: You can activate this orb to create a flash of bright light. All targets within Close range become Vulnerable until they mark HP.

entries.name: Death Tea
entries.description: After you drink this tea, you instantly kill your target when you critically succeed on an attack. If you don't critically succeed on an attack before your next long rest, you die.

entries.name: Mirror of Marigold
entries.description: When you take damage, you can spend a Hope to negate that damage, after which the mirror shatters.

entries.name: Stardrop
entries.description: You can use this stardrop to summon a hailstorm of comets that deals 8d20 physical damage to all targets within Very Far range.

entries.name: GOLD
entries.description:
Gold is an abstract measurement of how much wealth a character has, and is measured in handfuls, bags, and chests, with 10 handfuls to 1 bag, and 10 bags to 1 chest. When you have marked all of the slots in a category and you gain another gold reward in that category, mark a slot in the following category and clear all the slots in the current one.

For example, if you have 9 handfuls and gain another, you instead mark 1 bag and erase all handfuls. If you have 9 bags and gain another, you mark 1 chest and erase all bags.

You can't have more than 1 chest, so if all your Gold slots are marked, you'll need to spend some of your gold or store it somewhere else before you can acquire more.

Optional Rule: Gold Coins
If your group wants to track gold with more granularity, you can add coins as your lowest denomination. Following the established pattern, 10 coins equal 1 handful.

entries.name: RUNNING AN ADVENTURE
entries.description:
INTRODUCTION

The GM is responsible for guiding the narrative and mechanics, creating memorable encounters; planning exciting sessions; selecting, creating, and using GM moves; crafting a full campaign; running dynamic NPCs; and more.

GM GUIDANCE

These three sections provide a foundation to help you get the most out of this game. The "GM Principles" are your guiding star—when in doubt, return to these principles.

GM PRACTICES

■ CULTIVATE A CURIOUS TABLE
Follow what catches the players' interest to foster an environment of creative inquiry.

■ GAIN YOUR PLAYERS' TRUST
Act in good faith, follow through on your promises, admit your mistakes.

■ KEEP THE STORY MOVING FORWARD
Advance the story through escalating action, new information, or changing circumstances after every action roll, whether it succeeds or fails.

■ CUT TO THE ACTION
Skip past the boring bits. When a scene drags on, end it.

■ HELP THE PLAYERS USE THE GAME
Players have more fun when you help them understand the system.

■ CREATE A META CONVERSATION
Empower players to speak out of character, use safety tools, and ask for clarification.

■ TELL THEM WHAT THEY WOULD KNOW
Don't hide obvious details or important information from the players.

■ GROUND THE WORLD IN MOTIVE
An NPC's actions flow from their goals and desires.

■ BRING THE GAME'S MECHANICS TO LIFE
Set a good example of how fiction and mechanics work together to enhance the game experience.

■ REFRAME RATHER THAN REJECT
If a player's contribution conflicts with the fiction, work with them to reshape it.

■ WORK IN MOMENTS AND MONTAGES
When framing a scene, decide which beats should be savored and which shouldn't linger.

entries.name: GM PRINCIPLES
entries.description:
■ BEGIN AND END WITH THE FICTION
Use the fiction to drive mechanics, then connect the mechanics back to the fiction.

■ COLLABORATE AT ALL TIMES, ESPECIALLY DURING CONFLICT
The PCs are the protagonists of the campaign; antagonism between player and GM should exist only in the fiction.

■ FILL THE WORLD WITH LIFE, WONDER, AND DANGER
Showcase rich cultures, take the PCs to wondrous places, and introduce them to dangerous creatures.

■ ASK QUESTIONS AND INCORPORATE THE ANSWERS
Ensuring that the players' ideas are included results in a narrative that supports the whole group's creativity.

■ GIVE EVERY ROLL IMPACT
Only ask the players to roll during meaningful moments.

■ PLAY TO FIND OUT WHAT HAPPENS
Be surprised by what the characters do, the choices they make, and the people they become.

■ HOLD ON GENTLY
Don't worry if you need to abandon or alter something that came before.

entries.name: PITFALLS TO AVOID
entries.description:
■ UNDERMINING THE HEROES
If a roll doesn't go well, show how it was impacted by an adversary's prowess, environmental factors, or unexpected surprises, rather than the PC's incompetence.

■ ALWAYS TELLING THE PLAYERS WHAT TO ROLL
Let the players decide how to handle a challenge.

■ LETTING SCENES DRAG
Shake it up or cut away when a scene has concluded, the table's energy is flagging, or people are talking in circles.

■ SINGULAR SOLUTIONS
Don't get hung up on one right answer to a problem. If the players have a clever idea, make it work.

Overplanning
Spend your prep time inventing situations instead of scripting scenes. If the players surprise you, take a break to think through your options.

■ HOARDING FEAR
Spend Fear when you have the opportunity. The players will always generate more.

For more in-depth GM guidance, see pg. 140 of the Daggerheart Core Rulebook.

entries.name: CORE GM MECHANICS
entries.description:
ROLLING DICE

The GM has no Duality Dice; instead, they roll a single d20 called the GM's Die.

■ ADVERSARY ATTACK ROLLS

When an adversary attacks a PC, roll your d20 and add the adversary's attack bonus to the result. If the total meets or beats the target's Evasion, the attack succeeds; otherwise, the attack fails. On a successful attack, roll the attack's damage dice to determine how much it deals.

If you roll a natural 20 on an attack, your roll automatically succeeds and you deal extra damage. Roll damage normally, then add the highest number on the damage dice to the total. For example, an attack that deals 3d6+2 deals 18+3d6+2 on a critical success; the critical success does not affect the flat +2 bonus.

Note: a critical success on an adversary's reaction roll automatically succeeds, but confers no additional benefit.

entries.name: GUIDANCE ON ACTION ROLLS
entries.description:
After a player describes a move they want to make during the game, you might decide an action roll is necessary to determine how the scene progresses. Use this guide to determine what to present the player, choosing whichever option best fits the situation:

• Determine whether the roll is necessary, considering the PC's Experiences or backstory, the pressure they're acting under, and the possible outcomes.
• Establish the stakes of an action roll before the player makes it.
• Communicate any unavoidable consequences.
• If desired, you can offer the player the opportunity to forgo an action roll in exchange for agreeing to an interesting outcome, cost, or complication.

entries.name: MAKING MOVES
entries.description:
As the GM, you have GM moves that change the story in response to the players' actions. GM moves are found in specific spells or effects—when you make a GM move, you can describe the action in whatever way the fiction demands.

GM moves happen during GM turns. A GM turn begins when the spotlight passes to them and ends when the spotlight passes back to the players.

■ WHEN TO MAKE A MOVE

The GM can make a GM move whenever you want, but the frequency and severity depends on the type of story you're telling, the actions your players take, and the tone of session you're running.

Make a GM move when the players:
• Roll with Fear
• Fail an action roll
• Do something that has unavoidable consequences
• Give you a "golden opportunity" (an opening that demands an immediate response)
• Look to you for what happens next

entries.name: CHOOSING GM MOVES
entries.description:
The result of a player's action roll determines your response:

On a Critical Success, you let the player describe their success, then give them an additional opportunity or advantage.

On a Success with Hope, you let the player describe their success, then you show how the world reacts to it.

On a Success with Fear, you work with the player to describe their success, then take a Fear and make a GM move to introduce a minor consequence, complication, or cost:
• An adversary attacks
• The PC marks a Stress
• You introduce a new threat
• You raise the stakes of the conflict

entries.name: Example GM Moves
entries.description:
• Show how the world reacts
• Ask a question and build on the answer
• Make an NPC act in accordance with their motive
• Drive a PC to take action by dangling their goals in front of them
• Signal an imminent off-screen threat
• Reveal an unwelcome truth or unexpected danger
• Force the group to split up
• Make a PC mark Stress
• Make a move the characters don't see
• Show the collateral damage
• Clear an adversary's condition
• Shift the environment
• Spotlight an adversary
• Capture someone or something important
• Use a PC's backstory against them
• Take away an opportunity permanently

entries.name: QUICK REFERENCE: RESOLVING ACTION ROLLS
entries.description:
If you're unsure how to resolve a roll, think about these quick phrases:

Success with Hope: Yes, and... (You get what you want and gain a Hope.)

Success with Fear: Yes, but... (You get what you want, but there's a consequence, and the GM gains a Fear.)

Failure with Hope: No, but... (Things don't go as planned, but you gain a Hope.)

Failure with Fear: No, and... (Things don't go as planned and it gets worse. The GM gains a Fear.)

If the move you should make is not obvious from the fiction, draw inspiration from the "Example GM Moves" list:
• Introduce a new obstacle or enemy
• Ask the player what happens
• Have the PC mark a Stress
• Tell the players "everything is fine... for now."

entries.name: SOFT AND HARD MOVES
entries.description:
Soft moves go easier on the players—they give the party new information about the scene and offer them an opportunity to react to it. Hard moves are harsher, more impactful, or more direct—the PCs don't get an opening to interrupt, alter, or anticipate the outcome.

Use softer moves on rolls with Hope and harder moves on rolls with Fear.

entries.name: USING FEAR
entries.description:
You start a campaign with 1 Fear per PC in the party.

You gain Fear whenever a PC rolls with Fear, the PCs take a rest (see: Downtime), or when an ability says you do.

You can never have more than 12 Fear at one time.

Fear carries over between sessions.

Spend a Fear to:
• Interrupt the players to steal the spotlight and make a move
• Make an additional GM move
• Use an adversary's Fear Feature
• Use an environment's Fear Feature
• Add an adversary's Experience to a roll

entries.name: Fear Spending Guidelines
entries.description:
The dramatic tension of a scene correlates with the amount of Fear you spend during it. For guidance on how much Fear you should spend in a scene, consult the following table:

Incidental - A catch-up between PCs after an emotional/charged scene; gathering information; investigating a threat; minor resting during downtime. - 0-1 Fear

Minor - A brief sequence; a minor skirmish that introduces new foes or signals future trouble. - 1-3 Fear

Standard - A moderate challenge or notable objective; perilous travel that tests might and wit; a tense social encounter seeking crucial information or aid. - 2-4 Fear

Major - A large battle with a Solo or Leader adversary; a character-defining scene with a significant change to a character's personal story (such as revelation, growth, and betrayal). - 4-8 Fear

Climatic - A major confrontation with the villain of a story arc; resolving a character or a judicial duel to determine an important NPC's fate. - 6-12 Fear

If you find yourself with a large amount of Fear, consider:
• Spending Fast: Spend Fear before the players have a chance to react
• Spending Often: Spend Fear every time the spotlight swings to you
• Spending Big: Spend Fear to make multiple moves in a row
Spending Fear to make a move communicates the increased impact of your action. Fear moves often include one or more of these elements:
• Introducing new adversaries to a scene when their appearance hasn't been foreshadowed or lacks context.
• An adversary activating a powerful spell or transformation to deal massive damage or boost their capabilities.
• An environment exerting a strong negative effect on the party.

entries.name: Difficulty Benchmarks
entries.description:
The Difficulty of an attack roll or adversary is equal to the adversary's Difficulty score. The Difficulty of any other action made by the adversary is equal to the GM or the Difficulty score, plus (if applicable) the value of one of the adversary relevant Experience modifiers.

When a player makes an action roll without a specified Difficulty, the GM sets the Difficulty according to the totality of the circumstances. Refer to the following benchmark table for more guidance:

entries.name: Sprint Action Table
entries.description:
ROLL - SPRINT - LEAP - MANEUVER
5 - Sprint within Close range across an open field with an enemy present. - Make a running jump of half your height (about 3 feet for a human). - Walk slowly across a narrow beam.
10 - Sprint to Far range across an open field with an enemy present. - Make a running jump of your height (about 6 feet for a human). - Walk quickly across a narrow beam.
15 - Sprint within Close range across rough terrain with an enemy present. - Make a running jump of double your height (about 12 feet for a human). - Run across a narrow beam.
20 - Sprint within Close range through an active battle of multiple enemies. - Make a running jump of three times your height (about 18 feet for a human). - Run across a narrow beam in heavy wind.
25 - Sprint within Far range through a pitched battle in rough terrain. - Make a running jump of five times your height (about 30 feet for a human). - Run across a very narrow beam in an active rainstorm.
30 - Sprint across the heads of your enemies in a pitched battle. - Make a running jump of ten times your height (about 60 feet for a human). - Run across an inch-wide, oil-slicked beam in an active rainstorm.

entries.name: Lift Action Table
entries.description:
ROLL - LIFT - SMASH - GRAPPLE
5 - Lift a chair. - Destroy a glass cup. - Subdue a child.
10 - Lift a table or small chest. - Destroy a small wooden table. - Subdue a weak adult.
15 - Lift a grown person or large chest. - Break through a wooden door. - Subdue an average adult.
20 - Lift a horse, elk, ox, or a large chest up stairs. - Break through a stone wall. - Subdue a skilled wrestler.
25 - Lift a horse, elk, or a large monster. - Break through a dragon's teeth. - Subdue a large beast.
30 - Lift a falling portcullis gate. - Break a god's grip. - Subdue a legendary beast.

entries.name: Control Action Table
entries.description:
ROLL - CONTROL - HIDE - TINKER
5 - Ride a horse through easy terrain. - Evade notice under full cover on a moonless night. - Open a sticky lock with the appropriate key.
10 - Drive an ox-pulled cart. - Evade notice in limited cover on a moonless night. - Open a simple puzzle box.
15 - Ride a horse through rough terrain. - Evade notice in limited cover on an average night. - Disable a standard trap.
20 - Drive a cart through rough terrain. - Evade notice in the shadows on an average night. - Disable a complicated trap.
25 - Ride a wild horse through dangerous terrain. - Evade notice with minimal cover in ample light. - Open a door secured by a sequence of elaborate locks.
30 - Ride an enraged beast through dangerous terrain. - Evade notice with no cover in full daylight. - Disable an incredibly sensitive and deadly trap.

entries.name: Perceive Action Table
entries.description:
ROLL - PERCEIVE - SENSE - NAVIGATE
5 - Hear a loud noise twenty paces away. - Detect an obvious ambush or notice an obvious deception. - Follow a well-trod path in good lighting and weather.
10 - Hear a speaking voice fifty paces away. - Detect a looming threat or notice an average person's lies. - Follow an average path in good lighting and weather.
15 - Hear someone walking in the woods fifty paces away. - Detect hostile intent from a foe or see through a merchant's lies. - Follow a subtle path through rough conditions.
20 - Hear someone moving through the woods fifty paces away. - Detect a politician's veiled hostility or detect a nearby assassin. - Follow a subtle path through harsh conditions.
25 - Hear a prowling animal fifty paces away. - Identify a spymaster's plot or read a politician's true intentions. - Find your way with no path through dangerous conditions.
30 - Hear a diving bird a hundred paces away. - Sense a shred of doubt within a god's pronouncement. - Find your way through a trickery god's maze.

entries.name: Charm Action Table
entries.description:
ROLL - CHARM - PERFORM - DECEIVE
5 - Win the trust of a friendly neighbor. - Earn a meal from a friendly crowd. - Trick a trusting acquaintance.
10 - Win the trust of a friendly stranger. - Earn room and board in a small town or impress a small crowd. - Trick an average stranger.
15 - Win the trust of a cautious stranger or talk your way into a noble's party. - Earn room and board in a low-end tavern or impress a large crowd. - Trick an average merchant.
20 - Win the trust of a sympathetic foe or talk your way into an enemy's party. - Win lodging in a high-end tavern or impress a full theater. - Trick a trained courtier.
25 - Turn an enemy against their ruler or talk your way into a fat court. - Get your keep in a royal court or impress a full colosseum. - Trick a spymaster.
30 - Talk a hostile god into granting you a boon. - Save yourself from execution after offending the queen. - Trick a god.

entries.name: Recall Action Table
entries.description:
ROLL - RECALL - ANALYZE - COMPREHEND
5 - Recall uncommon facts about your community. - Unpack an obvious metaphor in a simple story. - Learn simple skills from an excellent teacher.
10 - Recall uncommon facts about a neighboring community. - Identify obvious subtext in a conversation. - Learn simple skills from an average teacher.
15 - Recall uncommon facts about a distant community. - Break an average cipher in a coded message. - Learn complicated skills from an excellent teacher.
20 - Recall specialized facts about a distant community. - Identify a weakness in a complicated battle plan. - Learn complicated skills under poor conditions.
25 - Recall specialized facts about a fallen kingdom. - Predict the downfall of a nation based on concealed misdeeds. - Learn complicated skills quickly under dangerous conditions.
30 - Recall secret information about an obscure historical group. - Identify the weakness in a divine interpreter's fighting form. - Learn complicated skills quickly from incomplete information.

entries.name: Giving Advantage and Disadvantage
entries.description: To viscerally convey how a PC's actions or circumstances affect their ability to act, grant them advantage (if, for instance, a creative action would lower an adversary's Difficulty by 3 or more) or disadvantage (by) instead of adjusting the Difficulty of an action roll.

entries.name: Adversary Advantage & Disadvantage
entries.description: If an adversary has advantage on an action roll, the GM rolls an extra d20 and counts only the higher result. If an adversary has disadvantage on an action roll, the GM rolls an extra d20 and counts only the lower result.

entries.name: Adversary Action Rolls
entries.description:
By default, adversaries don't normally make action rolls except for attack rolls and any unique actions described in their stat block. An adversary's action usually succeeds without an action roll; if you want an adversary's action to have a chance of failure, have any relevant PCs make a reaction roll instead.

However, for especially dramatic or difficult tasks that the PCs can't influence, you can give an adversary an action roll anyway. For an adversary's action roll, if the result is equal to or greater than the action's Difficulty, the action succeeds—otherwise it fails. You can spend a Fear before rolling to add 1d6 Experience from the adversary's stat block to the total. Use the same procedure when an adversary makes a reaction roll.

entries.name: Adversary Attacks
entries.description:
When an adversary attacks a PC, the GM rolls a d20 and adds the adversary's Attack Modifier to the result. If the total meets or beats the target's Evasion, the attack succeeds; otherwise, it fails. Before rolling, the GM can grant the attacking adversary advantage, impose disadvantage, or spend a Fear to add a relevant Experience from the adversary's stat block to the total.

On a success, the adversary deals the damage listed in their stat block to the target.

When an adversary's action lets the GM make an attack against multiple targets, they make one attack roll and compare it to each target's Evasion separately.

entries.name: Countdowns
entries.description:
Countdowns represent a period of time or series of events preceding a future effect. A countdown begins at a starting value. When a countdown advances, it's reduced by 1. The countdown's effect is triggered when the countdown reaches 0.

Note: You can track countdowns by "spinning down" dice or ticking off boxes.

Standard countdowns advance every time a player makes an action roll. If an adversary or environment ability refers to a "player's turn," then it means a standard countdown with a starting value of n.

Dynamic countdowns advance by up to 3 depending on the outcomes of action rolls. Consequence countdowns are dynamic countdowns tied to negative effects. Progress countdowns are dynamic countdowns to positive effects.

entries.name: Dynamic Countdown Advancement
entries.description:
Roll two d6s:

Failure with Fear - No advancement - Tick down 3
Failure with Hope - No advancement - Tick down 2
Success with Fear - Tick down 1 - Tick down 1
Success with Hope - Tick down 2 - No advancement
Critical Success - Tick down 3 - No advancement

entries.name: Advanced Countdown Features
entries.description:
Countdowns can have randomized starting values
• Loop countdowns that reset to their starting value after completing their effect once.
• Increasing countdowns that increase their starting value by 1 every time they loop.
• Decreasing countdowns that decrease their starting value by 1 every time they loop.
• Linked progress and consequence countdowns that simultaneously advance according to their separate outcomes.
• Long-term countdowns that advance after rests instead of action rolls.

entries.name: Arcane Hold
entries.description:
Choice: When the battle begins, choose a favored PC.
Trigger: The first time during a battle the favored PC is within Close range and hit with an attack.
Effect: Make an attack roll with a +6 modifier against the adversary. On a success, the target is temporarily Restrained by tendrils of powerful magic.

entries.name: Giving Out Gold, Equipment, and Loot
entries.description:
It's up to you and your players how much importance you want to place on gold, equipment, and loot in your campaign.

Adjust the availability and utility of wealth and equipment to reflect the tone, themes, and setting of your campaign.

If you don't wish to track gold, then when PCs go shopping for new items let them pick one or two from a short, preselected list that aligns with your campaign setting and the party's current location.

Otherwise, set the prices of goods and services by adjusting the entries in the Average Costs table to reflect your campaign setting:

Meals for a party of adventurers per night - 1 Handful
Standard inn room per night - 1 Handful
Luxury inn room per night - 1 Bag
Carriage ride - 2 Handfuls
Mount (horse, mule, etc.) - 3 Bags
Specialized tools - 3 Handfuls
Fine clothing - 3 Handfuls
Luxury clothing - 1 Bag
Tier 1 equipment (weapons, armor) - 1-5 Handfuls
Tier 2 equipment (weapons, armor) - 1-2 Bags
Tier 3 equipment (weapons, armor) - 5-10 Bags
Tier 4 equipment (weapons, armor) - 1-2 Chests

entries.name: Running GM NPCs
entries.description:
When you run NPCs as the GM, you should always strive to follow your GM principles and use them to bring the world to life. Differentiate NPCs with unique manners of speech and action; let their individual goals and desires motivate their actions.

The only essential elements for an NPC are their name, description, and motive. If it's likely that the NPC will take actions against them, give them a Difficulty. Adversaries can be invented or improvised by modifying the stat block of another adversary.

If an NPC becomes an ally in combat, they don't need a stat block—just put the spotlight on what they do and show how their involvement alters the fiction. NPCs that don't have Hit Points or Stress can still be injured or killed if the fiction demands it.

entries.name: NPC Feature Examples
entries.description:
VOLLEY OF ARROWS
Trigger: At battle begins and this NPC is involved.
Effect: Activate a countdown (Loop 3). It ticks down when a PC misses an attack. When it triggers, this NPC releases a volley of arrows at a target of the PCs' choice, dealing 2d10+3 physical damage.

MENTOR
Choice: When the battle begins, choose a protégé PC.
Trigger: Your protégé is within Close range and fails an attack roll.
Effect: Move into Melee range with the PC and give them advice or guidance. The next attack roll they make has advantage.

REGROUP
Choice: When a battle begins, choose a point within Far range.
Trigger: All PCs have marked all of their Armor Slots.
Effect: Teleport all PCs and this NPC to the chosen spot and clear one Armor Slot on each target.

INTO THE NIGHT
Trigger: The PCs start a long rest with this NPC.
Effect: Roll 1d4. On a 2 or less, this NPC steals 1 handful of gold from the party while they are sleeping, then disappears into the night.

entries.name: Optional GM Mechanics
entries.description:
FATE ROLLS
When the PCs want to leave an outcome entirely up to chance, they call for a fate roll. The GM establishes what's at stake and how big the risk is/and the likelihood. Then a player rolls one of their Duality Dice and interprets the result.

Examples:
"Roll your Fear Die. On a 4 or lower, the fire spreads beyond this house."

"I think it's really up to chance whether reinforcements will make it to you in time. Go ahead and roll your Fear Die—that'll determine the starting size of the countdown. When that triggers, reinforcements will arrive in your Far range."

"Go ahead and roll your Hope Die to see how big the crowd at the inn is tonight. The higher the roll, the bigger the crowd."

"Make a roll using your Hope Die to determine the number of Stamina Potions the shop has in stock."

entries.name: Falling and Collision Damage
entries.description:
If a character falls to the ground, you can use the following as a guide to determine the damage they take:

• A fall from Very Close range deals 1d10+3 physical damage.
• A fall from Close range deals 1d20+5 physical damage.
• A fall from Far or Very Far range deals 1d100+10 physical damage, or death at the GM's discretion.

If a character collides with an object or another character at a dangerous speed, they take 1d20+5 physical damage.

entries.name: Moving and Fighting Underwater
entries.description:
By default, attack rolls made while the attacker is underwater have disadvantage.

Characters can hold their breathe underwater; use a standard countdown (3) to track how long they can hold their breath. Advance the countdown whenever the PC takes an action. In addition if they fail a roll or with Fear while underwater, you can spend your GM move to advance the countdown one more time—or twice, if they rolled a failure with Fear.

Once the countdown ends, the underwater character will mark a Stress whenever they take an action.

entries.name: Conflict Between PCs
entries.description: Sometimes a player might want their character to act against another PC in the scene. Before jumping to rolling dice, discuss the situation with both players—see how to resolve the conflict. A roll might not be necessary to reach an outcome—but if rolling will be fun for everyone involved, come to a consensus on the terms of the roll, then facilitate the scene according to the results.

entries.name: Adversaries and Environments
entries.description:
USING ADVERSARIES

ADVERSARY STAT BLOCKS
All the information required to run an adversary is contained in their stat block. An adversary's stat block includes their:

NAME
Each stat block has a unique name. Abilities that affect adversaries of a certain name include all adversaries who use that stat block, regardless of their in-story name.

TIER
Each adversary is designed to oppose PCs of a certain tier. If you want to use an adversary with an adversary from another tier, adjust their stats.

TYPE
The adversary's type appears alongside their tier. An adversary's type represents the role they play in a conflict. The adversary types are:
• Bruisers: tough; deliver powerful attacks.
• Hordes: groups of identical creatures acting together as a single unit.
• Leaders: command and summon other adversaries.
• Minions: easily dispatched but dangerous in numbers.
• Ranged: fragile in close encounters but deal high damage at a range.
• Skulks: maneuver and exploit opportunities to ambush opponents.
• Socials: present challenges around conversation instead of combat.
• Solos: present a formidable challenge to a whole party, with or without support.
• Standards: representative of their fictional group.
• Supports: enhance their allies and disrupt their opponents.

DESCRIPTION
A summary of the adversary's appearance and demeanor.

MOTIVES & TACTICS
Suggested impulses, actions and goals for the adversary.

DIFFICULTY
The Difficulty of any roll made against the adversary, unless otherwise noted.

DAMAGE THRESHOLDS, HIT POINTS, AND STRESS
These systems function the same way they do for PCs. The

entries.name: Attack Modifier
entries.description: When you attack with the adversary, apply this bonus or penalty to your attack roll.

entries.name: Standard Attack
entries.description: A description of the adversary's primary mode of inflicting harm on the PCs. It includes the attack's name, its effective range, and the damage it deals on a success. Using an adversary's standard attack is a GM move.

entries.name: Experience (Optional)
entries.description: The GM can spend a Fear to add an adversary's relevant Experience to raise their attack roll or increase the Difficulty of a roll made against them.

entries.name: Example Experiences
entries.description:
Acrobatics - Hunt from Above - Navigation
Ambusher - Intimidation - Nobility
Bartering - Intrusion - Quick Reflexes
Blademaster - Keen Senses - Socialite
Bodyguard - Magical Knowledge - Stealth
Commander - Nature's Friend - Tracker

entries.name: Feature(s)
entries.description:
There are three kinds of adversary features: actions, reactions, and passives. Note: each adversaries stress is tracked individually. If a feature requires a GM to spend Stress to activate it, the Stress must come from the adversary whose feature is being activated. If a feature has a Fear requirement, it must be spent in addition to any Fear already spent—for instance, to interrupt the PCs and put the spotlight on the adversary.

• Actions: a special attack or other unique action that the adversary can perform when the spotlight is on them.
• Reactions: special effects that take effect when their trigger occurs, regardless of whether the spotlight is on the adversary.
• Passives: special abilities that remain in effect by default and require no resources or triggers to activate.

entries.name: Fear Feature(s)
entries.description: High-impact effects that cost a Fear to activate.

entries.name: Haymaker
entries.description: Action: Make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, deal X direct physical damage.

entries.name: Shredding Strike
entries.description: Action: Make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, deal X physical damage and the target must mark an Armor Slot, gaining its benefit (they can still use armor to reduce the damage).

entries.name: More Where That Came From
entries.description: Action: Summon three Jagged Knife Lackeys, who appear at Far range.

entries.name: Heavy Hitter
entries.description: Reaction: When this adversary deals damage with a standard attack, you can spend a Fear to gain a +X bonus to the damage roll.

entries.name: Team Up
entries.description: Reaction: When another adversary within Very Close range of this adversary deals X damage to a creature, you can spend a Stress to make a standard attack against that same creature. On a success, combine the damage.

entries.name: Momentum
entries.description: Reaction: When this adversary makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

entries.name: Horde (X)
entries.description: Passive: When the Horde has marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals X damage instead.

entries.name: Minion (X)
entries.description: Passive: This adversary is defeated when they take any damage. For every X damage a PC deals to this adversary, defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.

entries.name: Relentless (X)
entries.description: Passive: This adversary can be spotlighted up to X times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

entries.name: Slow
entries.description: Passive: When you spotlight this adversary and they don't have a token on their stat block, they can't act yet. Place a token on their stat block and describe what they're preparing to do. When you spotlight this adversary and they have a token on their stat block, clear the token and they can act.

entries.name: Arcane Form
entries.description: Passive: This adversary is resistant to magic damage.

entries.name: Armored Carapace
entries.description: Passive: When this adversary takes physical damage, reduce it by X.

entries.name: Explosion
entries.description: Action: Spend a Fear to erupt in a fiery explosion. Make an attack against all targets within Close range. Targets the adversary succeeds against take 1d8 magic damage and are knocked back to Far range.

entries.name: BUILDING BALANCED ENCOUNTERS
entries.description:
When planning a battle, start with [(3 x the number of PCs in combat) + 2] Battle Points and make the following adjustments:

-1 for an easier or shorter fight
-2 if you're using 2 or more Solo adversaries
-2 if you add +1d4 for a static +2) to all adversaries' damage rolls
+1 if you choose an adversary from a lower tier
+1 if you don't include any Bruisers, Hordes, Leaders, or Solos
+2 for a harder or longer fight

Then spend your Battle Points to add an adversary to the encounter:

Spend 1 point for each group of Minions equal to the size of the party.
Spend 1 point for each Social or Support adversary.
Spend 2 points for each Horde, Ranged, Skulk, or Standard adversary.
Spend 3 points for each Leader adversary.
Spend 4 points for each Bruiser adversary.
Spend 5 points for each Solo adversary.

entries.name: DEFEATED ADVERSARIES
entries.description: When an adversary marks their last Hit Point, they are defeated: incapacitated, tied up, routed, killed, or anything else the table decides makes sense.

entries.name: ADVERSARY STAT BLOCK BENCHMARKS
entries.description:
ADVERSARY STATISTIC | TIER 1 | TIER 2 | TIER 3 | TIER 4
Attack Modifier | +1 | +2 | +3 | +4
Damage Dice | 1d6+2 to 1d12+4 | 2d6+3 to 2d12+4 | 3d6+3 to 3d12+5 | 4d8+10 to 4d12+15
Difficulty | 11 | 14 | 17 | 20
Damage Thresholds | Major 7/Severe 12 | Major 10/Severe 20 | Major 20/Severe 32 | Major 25/Severe 45

entries.name: ACID BURROWER
entries.description:
Tier 1 Solo
A horrid insect with digging claws and acidic blood.
Motives & Tactics: Burrow, drag away, feed, reproduce

Difficulty: 12 | Thresholds: 8/15 | HP: 8 | Stress: 3
ATK: +3 | Claws: Very Close | 1d12+2 phy

Experience: Terror Strike +2

FEATURES
Relentless (3) - Passive: The Burrower can be spotlighted up to three times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.
Earth Eruption - Action: Mark a Stress to have the Burrower burst out from underground within Very Close range. The target must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or be knocked over, making them Vulnerable until they clear this.
Spit Acid - Action: Make an attack against all targets in front of the Burrower, out to Far range. Targets the Burrower succeeds against take 1d4 magic damage and are Vulnerable until they clear this condition.
Acid Bath - Reaction: When the Burrower takes Severe damage, all creatures within Very Close range must succeed on their acidic blood, taking 1d10 physical damage. This splash covers the ground within Very Close range with blood, and all creatures other than the Burrower who move through it take 1d6 physical damage.

entries.name: BEAR
entries.description:
Tier 1 Bruiser
A large beast with thick fur and powerful claws.
Motives & Tactics: Hunt, climb, defend territory, scavenge, track

Difficulty: 14 | Thresholds: 5/17 | HP: 7 | Stress: 2
ATK: +1 | Claws: Melee | 1d8+3 phy

Experience: Ambusher +1, Keen Senses +2

FEATURES
Overwhelming Force - Passive: Targets who mark HP from the Bear's attacks are knocked prone and Restrained until the target makes a successful Agility Reaction Roll.
Bite - Action: Mark a Stress to make an attack against a target within Melee range. On a success, deal 3d4+10 physical damage and the target is Restrained until they break free with a successful Strength Roll.
Momentum - Reaction: When the Bear makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

entries.name: CAVE OGRE
entries.description:
Tier 1 Solo
A massive humanoid who sees all support life as food.
Motives & Tactics: Bite off heads, feast, rip limbs, stomp, throw enemies

Difficulty: 13 | Thresholds: 8/15 | HP: 8 | Stress: 3
ATK: +1 | Club: Very Close | 1d10+7 phy

Experience: Throw +2

FEATURES
Ramp Up - Passive: You must spend a Fear to spotlight the Ogre. While spotlighted, they can make their standard attack against all targets in Close range.
Bone Breaker - Passive: The Ogre's attacks deal direct damage.
Hall of Boulders - Action: Mark a Stress to pick up heavy objects and throw them at all targets in front of the Ogre within Far range. Make an attack against these targets. Targets the Ogre succeeds against take 1d8+5 physical damage. If they succeed against more than one target, you gain a Fear.
Rampaging Fury - Reaction: When the Ogre marks 2 or more HP, they can rampage. Move the Ogre to a point within Close range and deal 2d6+3 direct physical damage to all targets in their path.

entries.name: CONSTRUCT
entries.description:
Tier 1 Solo
A roughly humanoid being of stone and steel, assembled and animated by magic.
Motives & Tactics: Destroy environment, serve creator, smash target, trample groups

Difficulty: 11 | Thresholds: 7/15 | HP: 9 | Stress: 4
ATK: +4 | Fist Slam: Melee | 1d20 phy

FEATURES
Relentless (2) - Passive: The Construct can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.
Weak Structure - Passive: When the Construct marks HP from physical damage, they must mark an additional HP.
Trample - Action: Mark a Stress to make an attack against all targets in the Construct's path when they move. Targets hit the Construct succeeds against take 1d8+3 physical damage.
Overload - Reaction: Before rolling damage for the Construct's attack, you can mark a Stress to gain a bonus to the damage roll equal to the Construct's current number of marked HP.
Death Quake - Reaction: When the Construct marks their last HP, they explode in a shower of stone and metal. Make an attack with advantage against all targets within Very Close range. Targets the Construct succeeds against take 1d12+2 magic damage.

entries.name: COURTIER
entries.description:
Tier 1 Social
An ambitious and ostentatiously dressed socialite.
Motives & Tactics: Discreetly gain favor, maneuver, scheme

Difficulty: 12 | Thresholds: 4/8 | HP: 3 | Stress: 1
ATK: -1 | Daggers: Melee | 1d4+2 phy

Experience: Socialite +3

FEATURES
Mockery - Action: Mark a Stress to say something mocking and force a target within Close range to make a Presence Reaction Roll (14) or be lost in their head thinking about that barb until they clear this.
Scapegoat - Action: Spend a Fear and target a PC. The Courtier convinces a crowd of prominent individuals that the target is the cause of their current conflict or misfortune.

entries.name: DEEPROOT DEFENDER
entries.description:
Tier 1 Bruiser
A burly vegetable person with grasping vines.
Motives & Tactics: Ambush, grab, protect, pummel

Difficulty: 11 | Thresholds: 8/14 | HP: 7 | Stress: 3
ATK: +2 | Vines: Close | 1d8+3 phy

Experience: Hunter +2

FEATURES
Ground Slam - Action: Slam the ground, knocking all targets within Very Close range back to Far range. Each target knocked back this way must mark a Stress.
Vine Grapple - Action: Mark a Stress to launch grasping vines within Close range. On a success, spend a Fear to pull them into Melee range, deal 1d6+2 physical damage, and Restrain them until the Defender takes Severe damage.

entries.name: DIRE WOLF
entries.description:
Tier 1 Skulk
A large wolf with menacing teeth, seldom encountered alone.
Motives & Tactics: Defend territory, harry, protect pack, surround, trail

Difficulty: 11 | Thresholds: 5/9 | HP: 4 | Stress: 1
ATK: +2 | Claws: Melee | 1d6+2 phy

Experience: Keen Senses +1

FEATURES
Pack Tactics - Passive: If the Wolf makes a standard attack and another Dire Wolf is within Melee range of the target, deal 1d8+5 physical damage instead of their standard damage and you gain a Fear.
Howling Strike - Action: Mark a Stress to make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, deal 3d4+10 direct physical damage and mark them Vulnerable until they clear at least 1 HP.

entries.name: GIANT MOSQUITOES
entries.description:
Tier 1 Horde (5/HP)
Dozens of fist-sized mosquitoes, flying together for protection.
Motives & Tactics: Fly away, harass, steal blood

Difficulty: 10 | Thresholds: 5/9 | HP: 5 | Stress: 1
ATK: -2 | Proboscis: Melee | 1d8+3 phy

Experience: Camouflage +2

FEATURES
Horde (1d4+1) - Passive: When the Mosquitoes have marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 1d4+1 physical damage instead.
Flying - Passive: While flying, the Mosquitoes have a +2 bonus to their Difficulty.
Blood Drain - Reaction: When the Mosquitoes' attack causes a target to mark HP, you can mark a Stress to force the target to mark an additional HP.

entries.name: GIANT RAT
entries.description:
Tier 1 Minion
A cat-sized rodent skilled at scavenging and survival.
Motives & Tactics: Burrow, hunger, scavenge, wear down

Difficulty: 10 | Thresholds: None | HP: 1 | Stress: 1
ATK: -1 | Claws: Melee | 1 phy

Experience: Keen Senses +3

FEATURES
Minion (2) - Passive: The Rat is defeated when they take any damage. For every 2 damage a PC deals to the Rat, defeat an additional Rat within range the attack would succeed against.
Group Attack - Action: Spend a Fear to choose a target and spotlight all Giant Rats within Close range of the target. Those Minions move into Melee range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 1 physical damage per defeated that damage.

entries.name: GIANT SCORPION
entries.description:
Tier 1 Bruiser
A human-sized arachnid with tearing claws and a stinging tail.
Motives & Tactics: Ambush, feed, grapple, poison

Difficulty: 13 | Thresholds: 7/13 | HP: 6 | Stress: 3
ATK: +1 | Pincers: Melee | 1d12+2 phy

Experience: Camouflage +2

FEATURES
Double Strike - Action: Mark a Stress to make a standard attack against two targets within Melee range.
Venomous Stinger - Action: Make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, spend a Fear to deal 1d4+4 physical damage and the target is Poisoned. If they already succeed on a Knowledge Roll (16). While Poisoned, the target must roll a d6 before they make an action roll. On a result of 4 or lower, they must mark a Stress.
Momentum - Reaction: When the Scorpion makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

entries.name: GLASS SNAKE
entries.description:
Tier 1 Standard
A clear serpent with a massive head that leaves behind a glass shard trail wherever they go.
Motives & Tactics: Climb, feed, keep distance, scare

Difficulty: 14 | Thresholds: 6/10 | HP: 5 | Stress: 3
ATK: +2 | Glass Fangs: Very Close | 1d8+2 phy

FEATURES
Armor-Shredding Shards - Passive: After a successful attack against the Snake within Melee range, the attacker must mark an Armor Slot. They gain its benefit but cannot use that piece of armor until they repair it.
Spinning Serpent - Action: Mark a Stress to make an attack against all targets within Very Close range. Targets the Snake succeeds against are knocked back to Far range and take 1d4 phy.
Spitter - Action: Spend a Fear to introduce a d6 Spitter Die. When the Snake is in the spotlight, roll this die. On a result of 5 or higher, all targets in front of the Snake within Far range must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or take 1d4 physical damage. The Snake can take the spotlight a second time this GM turn.

entries.name: HARRIER
entries.description:
Tier 1 Ranged
A nimble fighter armed with javelins.
Motives & Tactics: Throw, hunt, harry, skip, profit

Difficulty: 12 | Thresholds: 5/9 | HP: 3 | Stress: 3
ATK: +1 | Javelin: Close | 1d6+2 phy

Experience: Camouflage +2

FEATURES
Maintain Distance - Passive: After making a standard attack, the Harrier can move to a point within Far range.
Fall Back - Reaction: When a creature moves into Melee range to make an attack, you can mark a Stress before the attack roll to move anywhere within Close range and make an attack against that creature. On a success, deal 1d10+2 physical damage.

entries.name: ARCHER GUARD
entries.description:
Tier 1 Standard
A city guard with a longbow and quiver with arrows fletched in the settlement's colors.
Motives & Tactics: Arrest, close gates, make it through the day, pin down

Difficulty: 10 | Thresholds: 4/8 | HP: 3 | Stress: 2
ATK: +1 | Longbow: Far | 1d6+3 phy

Experience: Local Knowledge +2

FEATURES
Hobbling Shot - Action: Make an attack against a target within Far range. On a success, mark a Stress to deal 1d12+3 physical damage. If the target moves HP from this attack, they have disadvantage on Agility Rolls until they clear at least 1 HP.

entries.name: BLADED GUARD
entries.description:
Tier 1 Standard
An armored guard bearing a sword and shield painted in the settlement's colors.
Motives & Tactics: Arrest, close gates, make it through the day, pin down

Difficulty: 12 | Thresholds: 5/9 | HP: 5 | Stress: 2
ATK: +1 | Longsword: Melee | 1d6+1 phy

Experience: Local Knowledge +3

FEATURES
Shield Wall - Passive: A creature with tries to move within Very Close range of a Bladed Guard when one or more additional Bladed Guards are standing in a line alongside the first, and each is within Melee range of at least one other guard, the Difficulty increases by the total number of guards in that line.
Detain - Action: Make an attack against a target within Melee range. On a success, mark a Stress to Restrain the target until they break free with a successful Strength Roll.

entries.name: HEAD GUARD
entries.description:
Tier 1 Leader
An experienced guard with a mace, a whistle, and a bellowing voice.
Motives & Tactics: Arrest, close gates, take down, seek glory

Difficulty: 15 | Thresholds: 7/13 | HP: 7 | Stress: 3
ATK: +4 | Mace: Melee | 1d10+4 phy

Experience: Commander +2, Local Knowledge +2

FEATURES
Rally Guards - Action: Spend 2 Fear to spotlight the Head Guard and up to 2d4 allies within Far range.
On My Signal - Reaction: Choose a trigger. The GM will remember it. The first time that condition is met, the spotlight lights the countdown. If not, the spotlight can all allies the condition makes a standard attack with advantage against the nearest target they can attack. If any of them succeed, place a Fear.
Momentum - Reaction: When the Head Guard makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

entries.name: JAGGED KNIFE BANDIT
entries.description:
Tier 1 Standard
A cunning criminal in a cloak bearing one of the gang's iconic knives.
Motives & Tactics: Escape, profit, threaten

Difficulty: 12 | Thresholds: 5/14 | HP: 5 | Stress: 3
ATK: +1 | Daggers: Melee | 1d8+1 phy

Experience: Thief +2

FEATURES
Climber - Passive: The Bandit climbs just as easily as they run.
From Above - Passive: When the Bandit succeeds on a standard attack from above a target, they deal 1d10+1 physical damage instead of their standard damage.

entries.name: JAGGED KNIFE HEXER
entries.description:
Tier 1 Support
A staff-wielding bandit in a cloak adorned with magical paraphernalia, using curses to hex their foes.
Motives & Tactics: Command, hex, slice, sneak

Difficulty: 13 | Thresholds: 5/9 | HP: 4 | Stress: 4
ATK: +3 | Staff: Far | 1d6+2 mag

Experience: Magical Knowledge +2

FEATURES
Curse - Action: Choose a target within Far range and temporarily Curse them. Roll a d20. If the number rolled is higher than the target's current HP, the target is Cursed until they mark an additional HP using Hope to make the roll for with Fear instead.
Chaotic Ray - Action: Make an attack against all targets within Very Close range. Mark a Stress to deal 2d6+3 magic damage. All targets succeed their saves have advantage.

entries.name: JAGGED KNIFE KNEEBREAKER
entries.description:
Tier 1 Bruiser
A criminally armed with a large knife.
Motives & Tactics: Grapple, intimidate, profit, steal

Difficulty: 12 | Thresholds: 7/14 | HP: 7 | Stress: 3
ATK: -1 | Club: Melee | 1d4+6 phy

Experience: Thief +2, Unveiled Threats +3

FEATURES
Tie Got 'Em - Passive: Creatures Restrained by the Kneebreaker cannot benefit from Help actions by other adversaries.
Hold Them Still - Action: Make an attack against a target within Melee range. On a success, the target takes no damage but is Restrained and Vulnerable to all attacks. The Kneebreaker takes damage, with a successful Strength Roll or if need automatically if the Kneebreaker takes Major damage.

entries.name: JAGGED KNIFE LACKEY
entries.description:
Tier 1 Minion
Small-time cutthroats and small-minded cutthroats, eager to prove themselves.
Motives & Tactics: Impress, profit, throw money, sneak

Difficulty: 9 | Thresholds: None | HP: 1 | Stress: 1
ATK: -2 | Daggers: Melee | 2 phy

Experience: Thief +2

FEATURES
Minion (5) - Passive: The Lackey is defeated when they take any damage. For every 5 damage a PC deals to the Lackey, defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.
Group Attack - Action: Spend a Fear to choose a target and spotlight all Jagged Knife Lackeys within Close range of the target. Those Minions move into Melee range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 2 physical damage each. Combine this damage.

entries.name: JAGGED KNIFE LIEUTENANT
entries.description:
Tier 1 Leader
A seasoned bandit in quality leathers with a strong voice and cunning eyes.
Motives & Tactics: Bully, command, profit, reinforce

Difficulty: 13 | Thresholds: 7/14 | HP: 6 | Stress: 3
ATK: +2 | Javelin: Close | 1d8+3 phy

Experience: Local Knowledge +2

FEATURES
Tactics - Action: When you spotlight the Lieutenant, mark a Stress to also spotlight two allies within Far range.
More Where That Came From - Action: Summon three Jagged Knife Lackeys who appear at Far range.
Cloak and Daggers - Action: Mark a Stress to make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, deal 2d6+3 magic damage and the target must mark a Stress.
Vulnerable target within Close range. On a success, deal 2d6+12 physical damage and the target must mark a Stress.
Momentum - Reaction: When the Lieutenant makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

entries.name: JAGGED KNIFE SHADOW
entries.description:
Tier 1 Skulk
A bandit assassin wearing a wicked knife and using shadow magic to isolate targets.
Motives & Tactics: Ambush, conceal, divide, profit

Difficulty: 12 | Thresholds: 4/8 | HP: 3 | Stress: 3
ATK: +1 | Daggers: Melee | 1d4+4 phy

Experience: Intrusion +3

FEATURES
Backstab - Passive: When the Shadow succeeds on a standard attack that has advantage, they deal 1d8 physical damage instead of their standard damage.
Cloaked - Action: Become Hidden until after the Shadow's next attack. Attacks made while Hidden from this feature have advantage.

entries.name: JAGGED KNIFE SNIPER
entries.description:
Tier 1 Ranged
A sickly bandit striking from cover with a shortbow.
Motives & Tactics: Ambush, hide, profit, reposition

Difficulty: 11 | Thresholds: 4/7 | HP: 3 | Stress: 3
ATK: -1 | Shortbow: Far | 1d10+2 phy

Experience: Stealth +2

FEATURES
